questions.question: What type of operating systems do monolithic systems use?
questions.explanation: Monolithic operating systems are designed as a single, self-contained unit, with all components and processes running in the same kernel.

questions.question: Which type of operating system is a collection of small, independent programs that perform specific tasks?
questions.explanation: Device drivers are small programs that manage and control hardware devices in an operating system.

questions.question: What type of operating system is used in embedded systems?
questions.explanation: Real-time operating systems are designed to handle time-sensitive tasks and provide predictable performance.

questions.question: Which type of operating system is a combination of microkernel and monolithic operating systems?
questions.explanation: Hybrid operating systems combine elements of microkernel and monolithic operating systems to achieve improved performance and security.

questions.question: What type of operating system is designed to handle and manage hardware devices?
questions.explanation: Device drivers are small programs that manage and control hardware devices in an operating system.
